exercises.title: Hello, World!
exercises.instructions:
Write a JavaScript program that prints exactly:
Hello, World!

exercises.title: Store Your Name
exercises.instructions:
Create a variable called name and assign "Test" into it.
Then print: Hello, <name>!

exercises.title: Variable: Store and Print a Name
exercises.instructions: Create a variable called 'name' holding the string 'Ada' and print it.

exercises.title: Variable: Store a Number
exercises.instructions: Create a variable called 'age' holding the number 12 and print it.
exercises.hints: Numbers have no quotes around them

exercises.title: Variable: A Decimal Number
exercises.instructions: Create a variable called 'price' holding the number 9.5 and print it.
exercises.hints: Decimals are numbers with a decimal point

exercises.title: Simple Math
exercises.instructions:
Create two variables x = 10 and y = 3.
Print the result of: x + y, x - y, x * y, and x / y (each on a new line).

exercises.title: Variable: A Boolean
exercises.instructions: Create a variable 'isStudent' set to true and print it.
exercises.hints: Booleans are true or false — lowercase, no quotes

exercises.title: Variable: Add Two Numbers
exercises.instructions: Create a = 3 and b = 4, then print their sum.
exercises.hints: Use the + operator between the two variable names

exercises.title: Variable: Greeting String
exercises.instructions: Create name = 'Sam' and print 'Hello, Sam' using string concatenation.
exercises.hints: Use + to join two strings together

exercises.title: Variable: Type of a Value
exercises.instructions: Create x = 5 and print its type using typeof.
exercises.hints: typeof returns the type of a value as a string

exercises.title: Variable: Reassign
exercises.instructions: Set x = 10, then reassign x = 20, and print x.
exercises.hints: A variable keeps the last value assigned to it
